ddt:Target.Name: RC7_ACU_eng_v2.22
ddt:Target.Description:
2012 January, 12th V0.01: Database creation
2012 March, 26th V0.02: Modification of Data DTCDeviceIdentifier (add of 15-40 DTC compare to T4)
2012 March, 28th V0.03: Add of IO (SBR/Lamp/CrashOutput)
2012 March, 30th V0.04: Modification of : - conversion formula for loop impedance - conversion formula for sensors Add of Reading Direction Side Configuration Modification of sensors traceability services
2012 April, 13th V0.05: Modification of : - Output Control requests - Devices in coherence with DTCDevice Identifier - Reading of firing line configuration
2012 April, 19th V0.06: Creation of : - Impedance screen Update of all screen
2012 April, 19th V1.00: First official version - update of configuration services (reading / writting)
2012 July, 9th V1.01: - update of all services : modification of bit offset (7 -> 0, 6 -> 1, ...)
2012 July, 10th V1.02: - update device failure status - add item by item reading / writting configuration services - update of the screens
2012 September, 17th V2.00 - update service number in accordance with DED - first offcial version
2012 September, 18th V2.01 - Correction of configuration services (naming convention)
2012 October, 1éth V2.02 - Correction of data (CRD Front Left curtain airbag and CRD Front Right curtain airbag)
2012 October, 22th V2.03 - Correction of screen, ResetCrash request - Correction of data name (CRD_xxx => CRD xxx) - Correction of screens - Update conversion value for sensors
2012 November, 28th V2.04 - Correction of Configuration sensor request
2012 December, 03rd V2.05 - remove item by item reading / writting configuration services - add crash telegram services - modification of 'Reading of memorized DTC before crash' service 22A0A5 -> 22A0C0 - add DID EFB1, EFB2, EFB3, EFB4 services for vehicle configuration
2013 January, 24th V2.06 - Correction of crash telegram services name
2013 February, 01st V2.07 - Add of LLA firing lines
2013 February, 06th V2.08 - Update link to DDT2000 verification errors
2013 February, 12th V2.09 - Update of trames Bx services
2013 February, 21th V2.10 - Crash TG services correction - Add of passenger Seat Psosition sensor data
2013 March, 1th V2.11 - Add HFE project
2013 May, 17th V2.12 - State synthesis of ECU screen modification - CrashOutput OutputControl service modification - Configuration sensor screen correction - Add link to SnapShot service for each DTC - Add FaultType $96 to Crash sensors DTC and deletion of $4A and $49 - Add FaultType $29 to the DTC $80D2 - Add DTC/requests/data/screens for FRSU - Add data Selection of inhibable passenger line in requests $22A002 and $22A003 - Deletion of $2EA012 - Add the detection and programmation data of the satellites in $22A009 - Update of trames Bx services (length modification) - Update the length of the ECUSerialNumber from 11 to 20 for $22A084 - Delete DTC $908155 - Add of $1060 and $1101 services - Add of test screen for internal failure reproductibility - Update Crash Telegram services (from service $22 to $31) and contains - Add of DTC $9082 (AECU arming error) - Update of $22A002 and $22A003 (Flag of Sensor configuration position) - Add of "CP Passenger AirbagON lamp management" in sensor configuration request (read and Write) - Update IO Control services
2013 September, 13th V2.13 - Update of reading Snapshot service ($1904)
2013 October, 3th V2.14 - Add AB ON Lamp configuration on the screens - Divide per 100 the MileAge data - Add others 1540 projects
2014 February V2.15 - Change 1902FF to 1902B9
2014 April V2.16 - Add EDR services and data
2016 April V2.17 - DID Errors correction - Add VDIAG $08 for SWP82
2016 April V2.18 -Auto identification correction
2016 May V2.19 -Auto identification correction
2016 September V2.21 - Correction DID A008 & A009
2018 January V2.22 - Correction DID A008 & A009: Added mnemonique in order to solve TradConf errors

Data.Name: CLEDEV
Data.Comment: ask to UET passive safety 65620 for this code.

Data.Name: CLEDEV For reset crash
Data.Comment: This key is used to allow the reset of the calculator

Data.Name: CLEDEV For crash_output
Data.Comment: This key is used to allow the sending of crash-output - Ask to passive safety UET (65620)

Data.Name: programmingOperationFingerPrint
Data.Comment: Only for programmable ECU
Data.Description:
The value is written by the ECU when executing the SendKey SecurityAccess service

The value can be read in boot loader mode.

Data.Name: vehicleManufacturerSparePartNumber
Data.Comment: Ordered Part Number - This value shall contain the number of the part ordered by OEM
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer spare part number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: systemSupplierIdentifier
Data.Comment: Name and address information of the supplier.
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier name and address information.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: VIN
Data.Comment: Vehicle Identification Number
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the VIN number.

Record data content and format shall be specified by the vehicle manufacturer.

It shall not be erased when the ECU is reprogrammed.

Data.Name: vehicleManufacturerECUHardwareNumber
Data.Comment: Hardware Number
Data.Description:
This value shall be used by reading services to reference the vehicle-manufacturer-specific ECU (server) hardware number.

Record data content and format shall be serverspecific and defined by vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: systemSupplierECUSoftwareNumber
Data.Comment: Software Number (Supplier)
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier specific ECU (server) software number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: systemSupplierECUSoftwareVersionNumber
Data.Comment: Software Version (Supplier)
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier specific ECU (server) software version number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: Serial number for center frontal restraints sensor
Traceability frame for the Airbag calculator.Year of manufacturing
Data.Comment: Year of manufacturing

Data.Name: Traceability frame for the Airbag calculator.Day of manufacturing
Data.Comment: jour de fabrication de l'UCE

Data.Name: Traceability frame for the Airbag calculator.ECUSerialNumber
Data.Comment: ECU serial number
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the ECU (server) serial number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific.

Data.Name: Side direction configuration
Data.Comment: Configuration du côté de direction

Data.Name: diagnosticVersion
Data.Comment: VDIAG
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the diagnostic version of the ECU.

It is defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Refer to manufacture ECU designer.

Data.Name: OutputPermanentControlList
Data.Comment: List of Data Identifiers for Input Output Control - The list defined here is AN EXAMPLE.
Data.Description:
Definition of IOControl

Data: IOCTL.DataIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all IO devices that may be
  controlled by InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
 
    0 - DID_Pump
    1 - DID_Wiper

If some of these IO devices have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the IO devices followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: DID_Wiper.Mode
     Type: List
       0 - Slow speed
       1 - High speed

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the InputOutpuControl request, after the IOCTL.OptionRecord.Parameter data.

Data.Comment: Input Output Control State status

Data.Comment: Input Output control parameter list

Data.Comment: Input Output Control State status

Data.Name: IOCTL.StatusRecord.Parameter
Data.Comment: Input Output control status

Data.Comment: DTC count

 - Resolution = 1ms/Digit

Data.Comment: DTC format - Renault/Nissan use value 0 (3 bytes DTC)

Data.Name: RoutineIdentifierList.CrashTelegramRequest
Data.Comment: Routine identifier for crash telegram request
Data.Description:
Definition of RoutineControl

Data: RoutineIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all routine identifier that may be used with InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
  
    0x4501 - ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave

If some of these routine identifer have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the routine identifer followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Min
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Max
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Freq

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the RoutineControl request, after the RoutineIdentifierList data.

Data.Name: RoutineIdentifierList.CrashTelegramAnswer
Data.Comment: Routine identifier for crash telegram answer
Data.Description:
Definition of RoutineControl

Data: RoutineIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all routine identifier that may be used with InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
  
    0x4501 - ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave

If some of these routine identifer have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the routine identifer followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Min
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Max
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Freq

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the RoutineControl request, after the RoutineIdentifierList data.

 - 255: Squib was not deployed, firing current counter is not available.
 - Resolution = 32us/Digit

Data.Name: DTCSnapshot.Mileage
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData Mileage (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: IOControlList
Data.Comment: List of Data Identifiers for Input Output Control - The list defined here is AN EXAMPLE.
Data.Description:
Definition of IOControl

Data: IOCTL.DataIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all IO devices that may be
  controlled by InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
 
    0 - DID_Pump
    1 - DID_Wiper

If some of these IO devices have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the IO devices followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: DID_Wiper.Mode
     Type: List
       0 - Slow speed
       1 - High speed

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the InputOutpuControl request, after the IOCTL.OptionRecord.Parameter data.

Data.Name: CT_Calibration_Byte
Data.Comment: ISA Algorithm Calibration Identification Number

Data.Name: CT_AlgorythmCalibration_Byte
Data.Comment: ISA Algorithm Calibration Identification Number

Data.Name: RoutineIdentifierList.CrashCurveRequest_InternalSensor
Data.Comment: Routine identifier for crash curve request
Data.Description:
Definition of RoutineControl

Data: RoutineIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all routine identifier that may be used with InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
  
    0x4501 - ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave

If some of these routine identifer have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the routine identifer followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Min
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Max
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Freq

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the RoutineControl request, after the RoutineIdentifierList data.
